content: We can frame the vocabulary learning of 'reflexion' as an iterative Self-Refine process: when a language learner first encounters the word 'reflexion', their initial understanding of its meaning and usage acts as a raw LLM output, which they can continuously refine through repeated self-reflection on how the term is used in different contexts (e.g. AI agent terminology vs. high school English reading passages). This creates a closed-loop learning framework that turns passive vocabulary memorization into active, self-improving knowledge construction.
